name: Hawaii
tagline: A new state CIO of Native Hawaiian ancestry, a $500M+ broadband and undersea-fiber buildout, and an AI roadmap for state agencies — all atop the most strategically significant U.S. defense footprint in the Pacific.

execSummary.summary: Hawaii budgets on a two-year (biennial) cycle and runs roughly a $20B all-funds operating budget, with about $10.6B in general funds. Technology spend is not a single line item — it sits with the Office of Enterprise Technology Services (ETS) and is distributed across agencies and the University of Hawaii. Three threads matter most for a Dell seller. First, leadership: Christine Sakuda became State CIO and ETS administrator in 2025, the state's first female CIO of Native Hawaiian ancestry, and is driving a practical AI roadmap for agencies. Second, connectivity: the Connect Kākou initiative has $500M+ in federal, state, and private funds, including a ~$120M inter-island undersea fiber system (Hawaiian Islands Fiber Link) targeting service in late 2026 and ~$149.5M in BEAD funding. Third, context: Hawaii hosts U.S. Indo-Pacific Command (INDOPACOM), Pearl Harbor, and DISA Pacific — a dense federal/defense buyer base adjacent to (but separate from) the state market. Buying runs through the State Procurement Office and NASPO ValuePoint cooperative contracts; the University of Hawaii is a large independent research-computing and broadband buyer.
opportunities: Agency AI adoption under the ETS roadmap — governed, auditable AI for permitting, disaster response, and service delivery (private/on-prem for sensitive workloads).
Broadband and inter-island connectivity buildout (Connect Kākou / HIFL) — networking, edge compute, and resilient infrastructure across six islands.
Disaster-resilience and recovery modernization in the wake of the 2023 Maui wildfires — secure, redundant compute and data protection.
Endpoint, client, and lifecycle refresh across state agencies, the Department of Education, and the University of Hawaii.

stats.label: Inter-island undersea fiber
stats.note: Hawaiian Islands Fiber Link; RFS late 2026

budget.intro: Hawaii budgets on a two-year (biennial) cycle; the Governor submits in December and fiscal years begin July 1. The FY2025–27 biennium runs roughly $20B all-funds per year (~$20.4B in FY2027), with about $10.6B in general funds. There is no single statewide 'IT budget' line — technology spend is distributed across agencies, ETS, the Department of Education, and the University of Hawaii, plus a set of named, largely federally funded connectivity programs.
notes: Budget figures are annual within a two-year (biennial) appropriation; confirm the enacted vs. supplemental figure before forecasting.
Most large connectivity dollars are federally sourced and flow through the University of Hawaii Broadband Office, not a state IT agency.

initiatives.name: Connect Kākou — Statewide Broadband
initiatives.summary: Connect Kākou is the Green administration's flagship broadband program with $500M+ in federal, state, and private funds. It includes ~$149.5M in BEAD funding (final federal approval to draw down, Jan 2026) to serve ~7,000 locations, ~82% via fiber. The University of Hawaii Broadband Office oversees implementation and contracts. A multi-island connectivity buildout creates demand for networking, edge compute, and resilient infrastructure.

initiatives.name: Hawaiian Islands Fiber Link (HIFL)
initiatives.summary: A ~$120M open-access, carrier-neutral inter-island undersea fiber system linking Oahu, Hawaii Island, Maui, Kauai, Lanai, and Molokai. UH awarded the build contract in 2025; the 24-fiber-pair system has a 25-year design life and targets ready-for-service in late 2026. It addresses Hawaii's core constraint — island isolation and single-points-of-failure — and pairs naturally with redundant data infrastructure and disaster-resilient compute.

initiatives.name: ETS AI Roadmap & Generative AI Governance
initiatives.summary: ETS is required to report to the Legislature on generative-AI risks and benefits and is standing up data-sharing/governance working groups. CIO Christine Sakuda is driving a practical AI roadmap spanning permit processing, disaster response, and service delivery. Agencies need governed, auditable AI — an opening for private/on-prem and private-cloud AI on sensitive data rather than open public models.

initiatives.name: Disaster Resilience & Recovery Modernization
initiatives.summary: Following the 2023 Maui wildfires and as a climate-exposed island state, Hawaii prioritizes resilient, redundant systems and rapid recovery. The Governor's supplemental budget emphasizes infrastructure and climate resiliency. This favors secure, redundant compute, backup/DR, and data-protection offerings — and reinforces the case for distributed infrastructure across islands.

initiatives.name: Federal / Defense Adjacency (INDOPACOM)
initiatives.summary: Hawaii hosts U.S. Indo-Pacific Command, Pearl Harbor Naval Shipyard, Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam, and the DISA Pacific data center. The state carries roughly 8% of the military's construction budget — the highest share of any state — including a $1.2B Pearl Harbor dry dock. This is a separate federal buying motion from the state market, but a major regional tailwind for Dell Federal/ISG and a reason to coordinate state and federal account teams.

procurement.intro: The State Procurement Office (SPO) is the central authority on procurement statutes and rules for the State and its counties. Technology purchasing commonly runs through SPO price-and-vendor-list contracts and the Cooperative Purchasing Program, which leverages NASPO ValuePoint and other multi-state cooperatives. The Office of Enterprise Technology Services (ETS) sets IT governance, the statewide IT portfolio/roadmap, and the IT budget/spend-request cycle that agencies follow.

vehicles.name: NASPO ValuePoint (via SPO)
vehicles.detail: Primary cooperative path for IT — multi-state pre-competed contracts. Confirm Dell agreements and Hawaii participation/resellers are current and correctly scoped.

vehicles.name: SPO Price & Vendor List Contracts
vehicles.detail: State-established contracts that agencies and counties buy from without running their own solicitation.

vehicles.name: Cooperative Purchasing Program
vehicles.detail: Piggyback purchasing under HRS 103D / HAR 3-128; jurisdictions must comply to use cooperative contracts.

entities.name: ETS (Office of Enterprise Technology Services)
entities.role: Central IT governance, statewide IT portfolio/roadmap, AI policy, and the IT budget/spend-request cycle.

entities.name: State Procurement Office (SPO)
entities.role: Central procurement authority for the State and counties; owns cooperative and price-list contracts.

entities.name: University of Hawaii (UH)
entities.role: Large independent research-computing buyer; runs the UH Broadband Office and Connect Kākou / HIFL contracts.

entities.name: Dept. of Education / Health
entities.role: Among the largest agency IT and modernization spenders.

procurement.competition: Hawaii is a relationship- and cooperative-driven market with a small in-state vendor base and high logistics/connectivity costs. Differentiation comes from resilient, redundant, island-aware infrastructure, AI-roadmap alignment with ETS, NASPO-ready offerings, and the ability to coordinate across state, UH, and the large adjacent federal/defense footprint rather than price alone.

powerStructure.name: Josh Green, M.D.
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: Champions Connect Kākou broadband and climate/disaster resiliency; sets the modernization agenda.

powerStructure.name: Christine Sakuda
powerStructure.title: State CIO & ETS Administrator
powerStructure.note: Appointed 2025; first female CIO of Native Hawaiian ancestry. Owns statewide IT direction and the AI roadmap.

powerStructure.name: Sylvia Luke
powerStructure.title: Lieutenant Governor
powerStructure.note: Public face of broadband expansion announcements and federal-funding milestones.

powerStructure.name: University of Hawaii / UH Broadband Office
powerStructure.title: Implementation lead
powerStructure.note: Oversees Connect Kākou implementation and the HIFL undersea-fiber and BEAD contracts.

powerStructure.name: State Procurement Office (SPO)
powerStructure.title: Central procurement authority
powerStructure.note: Owns cooperative/NASPO and price-list contracts — the path to most state IT buys.

news.title: Hawaii gets final federal OK to spend ~$149.5M (BEAD) on statewide internet
news.impact: Connect Kākou moves from approval to execution; networking and infrastructure demand across ~7,000 locations.

news.title: UH inter-island undersea fiber network still targeting 2026 completion
news.impact: HIFL build proceeds toward late-2026 ready-for-service; redundant island connectivity nears reality.

news.title: Federal AI executive order signals preemption of state AI laws
news.impact: National AI standard may reshape how far Hawaii's own AI governance can go; agencies still need governed AI now.

news.title: CIO Sakuda keynotes Hawaii AI & Cloud Innovation Summit on the state's AI roadmap
news.impact: ETS lays out a practical path for AI in permitting, disaster response, and services — a buying signal for governed AI.

news.title: Contractors selected to install the ~$120M HIFL undersea fiber system
news.impact: Major construction milestone for inter-island connectivity backbone.

news.title: Hawaii Senate advises and consents on Christine Sakuda as State CIO
news.impact: Stable, AI-forward leadership at ETS — the key relationship for the statewide tech motion.

dellActions: Build the ETS relationship around the AI roadmap — position governed, private/on-prem and private-cloud AI for sensitive agency workloads (permitting, disaster response, services).
Engage the University of Hawaii and UH Broadband Office on Connect Kākou / HIFL — networking, edge compute, and resilient infrastructure for a six-island buildout.
Lead with disaster-resilience and data-protection messaging — redundant, island-aware compute and backup/DR resonate post-Maui and in a climate-exposed state.
Validate Dell's NASPO ValuePoint coverage and Hawaii SPO contract/reseller scope before pursuing agency deals.
Coordinate state and federal account teams — the INDOPACOM / Pearl Harbor / DISA Pacific footprint is a major adjacent opportunity and a credibility anchor.
